Of the benefit the business of the
city has received from the land
openings" said leading Sprague
avenue hotel keeper this morning.
Every man in the business of sup
plying public wants, from the boot
black to the proprietor of the larg-
est hotel in the city, has derived
some benefit from the registration
rush. think the total left in the
city by strangers will amount to
$1,000,000. The beauty of it all is
that this business came without
costing Spokane a dollar, and will
in the end be one of the greatest
advertisements the city has ever
had"


Next week, it is expected that
Spokane will see the greatest
crowd of strangers she has ever
witnessed within her borders. In
addition to the thousands that will
come for the irrigation congress,
many thousands more who entered
in the land openings, and then
went to Seattle or elsewhere in
the northwest. will return, to be
here for the dual attractions of
the land drawings and the irriga.
ton meeting.
